meta.title: Enterprise CMS Migration: Legacy to Headless
meta.excerpt: How a financial services firm reduced content publishing time by 80%

props.text: A mid-size financial services company was struggling with their legacy CMS. Content updates took days, required developer intervention, and the system couldn't support their multi-channel strategy. With a growing digital presence across web, mobile app, and partner portals, they needed a solution that could serve content to multiple platforms from a single source.

steps.title: Marketing creates content
steps.description: Content drafted in Word documents, sent via email

steps.title: Developer formats and uploads
steps.description: Manual HTML conversion, image optimization, upload to legacy CMS

steps.title: Production deployment
steps.description: Manual deployment process, scheduled for weekly release window

steps.title: Post-publish fixes
steps.description: Typos and formatting issues discovered after launch

props.title: The Pain Points
props.description: Legacy CMS created bottlenecks across the entire content pipeline

stats.context: From content ready to live on site
All content changes required developer time

props.text: We migrated to a headless Strapi CMS with a custom content workflow, enabling marketing to publish directly while maintaining quality controls. The new architecture decoupled content from presentation, allowing a single content source to serve the website, mobile app, and partner portals simultaneously.

beforeItems: Marketing creates content in Word doc

steps.title: Phase 1: Discovery
steps.description: Audit existing content, identify content types, map workflows

steps.title: Phase 2: Strapi Setup
steps.description: Configure content types, build editorial workflow, set up preview

steps.title: Phase 3: Migration
steps.description: Content migration scripts, image optimization, URL mapping

steps.title: Phase 4: Training
steps.description: Marketing team training, documentation, support processes

steps.title: Phase 5: Go-Live
steps.description: Phased rollout, monitoring, legacy system decommission

props.title: Key Technical Decisions
props.body: Hosting: Railway for Strapi (auto-scaling, managed PostgreSQL). Frontend: Next.js on Vercel (edge caching, instant deployments). Migration: Custom scripts vs manual - automated 80%, manual review 20%. Training: 2-day workshop + written guides + video tutorials. Rollout: Soft launch with 10% traffic for 2 weeks before full cutover.

props.title: Business Impact Beyond Cost Savings
props.body: Marketing team now publishes 4x more content without additional headcount. Mobile app launched with same content as website (previously impossible). Partner portal integration enabled new revenue stream (£120k/year). Content experiments and A/B testing now possible (previously blocked by developer availability).

props.text: Headless CMS pays for itself in developer time savings alone. The content velocity increase is a bonus. When evaluating a migration, focus on the hidden costs of your current system: developer time wasted on content changes, marketing team blocked by technical bottlenecks, and opportunities lost because you can't move fast enough. A modern headless CMS isn't just a technology upgrade—it's a business capability multiplier.
